resources.string: Wait time before a long press is recognized. A lower value makes long press more responsive, while a higher value requires a longer press.
Automatically capitalizes the first letter when starting to type in an empty text field.
Automatically capitalizes the next letter after typing a period followed by a space (end of sentence).
Double tap space to insert a period followed by a space and capitalize the next letter.
Turns “word - next” into a typographic dash while leaving line-start hyphens alone.
Automatically disable Alt or Alt-Lock when pressing Space.
Alternative methods to delete characters to the right of cursor
Hint: Ctrl + key can be configured in Nav Mode settings
When text is selected, these alternatives are disabled and Backspace deletes the selection.
Distance required to move cursor one position. Lower values make the cursor move faster.
Enable Alt+Ctrl keyboard shortcut to start speech recognition
Always show a fixed set of utility keys in the variation row instead of context-based variations.
Use dynamic variations and suggestions only.
Switch the top static row between default symbols and an alternative bracket/special-character set. Shift and Alt layers stay independent.
When Shift/Alt layer is latched by double tap, keep it active after selecting a symbol from that layer.
Settings are automatically saved and immediately applied to the input method.
Physical keyboard with long press support
1. Click the button below to open settings
3. Come back here and test the keyboard
Connect a physical keyboard and test below:
• Long press (500ms): inserts the Alt character
Type here with the physical keyboard…
Personalize emoji mappings for the SYM keyboard. Tap on any key to change its emoji.
Select which substitutions to use for auto-correction. The system language is shown at the top.
At least one substitution required for operation
Tap on a correction to edit it, or use the + button to add a new one.
No corrections available. Tap the + button to add one.
Enter a code and name for your custom substitution (e.g., \"fr\" / \"French\", \"mydict\" / \"My Dictionary\")
Maximum number of character differences allowed for auto-correction. 0 = disabled, 1-3 = increasing tolerance.
Filter unlikely typos based on key distance (QWERTY/AZERTY/QWERTZ)
Rank suggestions by edit type (insert > substitute > delete)
Automatically close SYM layout after inserting a character, pressing Alt, or pressing Enter. Does not close with Control or Shift.
Show the emoji SYM layout when cycling.
Show the symbols SYM layout when cycling.
Show the clipboard history when cycling.
Show the full emoji picker when cycling.
Use about 1.5x height for the emoji search page; other SYM pages keep their normal height.
Set the exact cycle order for all SYM pages by dragging each row.
Tap on any key below to change its emoji. Changes are saved automatically.
Are you sure you want to reset all SYM mappings to default? This action cannot be undone.
Choose what long press does: Alt+key, Shift+key, character variations, or Sym mappings
System sounds and notifications follow silent mode and Do Not Disturb.
Could not import this sound pack. Use a ZIP with audio files in normal/, space/, backspace/, enter/, and modifier/ folders.
Open the import guide on pastiera.eu
Select your keyboard layout. ALT, SYM and Ctrl mappings remain based on physical key position.
Convert letter keys to match the selected layout
Unable to load this layout mapping.
No key mappings found for this layout.
OFF: use one manually selected layout for all locales.
Show a toast when switching languages/layouts.
Manual override for device-specific key mappings.
Symbol inserted by dedicated hardware currency keys.
Quick launching, SYM shortcuts, and launcher key assignments
These settings share one launcher-key assignment list. Choose where Pastiera should listen for those assigned keys.
Use assigned launcher keys directly on the Android launcher or home screen.
Tap a key to assign or replace a command. Assigned keys are shared by both trigger modes.
Tap a key to assign or replace a command. Quick launcher is currently assigned to %1$s.
New: SYM can open the quick launcher. Pastiera would normally assign it to Space, but Space already has a shortcut. Open the assignments page and choose “Open quick launcher” on any key to try it.
Type to search. Enter opens the top result.
Launch automatically when only one result remains
When the quick launcher search leaves only one app, launch it without pressing Enter.
Do not render the full alphabetical app list. The quick launcher stays empty until you type and then shows only the top three matches.
When enabled, the QuickLauncher can also open while the cursor is inside an app text field.
In text fields, hold SYM and press Space. SYM latch cannot trigger this because tapping SYM cycles the SYM pages.
When enabled, search input follows the current Pastiera layout instead of Android’s raw QWERTY key output.
Also match app names with small typing mistakes. Existing prefix and abbreviation matches still rank higher.
Choose whether the shortcut opens Pastiera search or Niagara Launcher search. Niagara falls back to Pastiera if unavailable.

Results are ranked in this order: exact app name, app name prefix, word prefix, abbreviation/subsequence match, typo-tolerant app name match, then package-name match. Typo tolerance is ignored for very short queries.
Launcher provider, search/ranking rules, and SYM+Space from text fields.
Width, pill mode, shown command sources, favorites, aliases, and entry colors.
Open as a compact search pill and expand only after typing.
Press SYM and then an assigned key to launch apps or actions.
A new version of Pastiera is available (%1$s)
How Ctrl, mappings, app shortcuts, and layout hints work together.

Nav Mode turns letter keys into navigation and shortcut keys. The result can differ by app because some apps understand Android editor actions, while others only react to real Ctrl shortcuts.
Ctrl-hold navigation When Ctrl-hold navigation is off, physically held Ctrl is passed to the app whenever possible. Shortcuts such as Ctrl+B, Ctrl+I, or Ctrl+Z can then work directly in editors.
When Ctrl-hold navigation is on, physically held Ctrl first uses the Nav Mode mappings below. A mapped key performs its configured Nav Mode command.
Double-tap Ctrl Double-tapping Ctrl latches Nav Mode. After that, pressing a letter triggers the Nav Mode mapping until Nav Mode is left again. This is useful for arrows, Page Up/Down, Escape, Tab, and selection commands without holding Ctrl.
Action vs. Native Ctrl Actions such as Copy, Paste, Cut, Select All, and Undo are sent as Android editor actions. They work in many text fields, but not every app supports every action.
Use Native Ctrl when an app expects a real shortcut. For example, mapping I to Native Ctrl sends Ctrl+I, so editors such as Google Docs can use it for italic text. Undo can also be mapped as Native Ctrl if an app handles Ctrl+Z better than Android Undo.
QWERTZ on physical QWERTY Nav Mode uses the physical key position so navigation remains stable. Layout hints on keys show when the active text layout would treat that physical key as a different letter, such as Y/Z on QWERTZ.
Layout-aware app Ctrl shortcuts When held Ctrl is passed to apps, this optional setting can translate Ctrl+letter through the active Pastiera layout first. It affects app shortcuts, not the physical Nav Mode grid.
Overrides Each key can be configured as Keycode, Action, Native Ctrl, or None. Use overrides to solve app-specific conflicts without changing the whole mode.
Double-tap Ctrl to activate navigation mode
When enabled, text fields use Nav Mode mappings while Ctrl is held. When off, Ctrl shortcuts are passed to the app. Keys set to Native Ctrl are always passed through as real Ctrl shortcuts.
When held Ctrl is passed to apps, translate Ctrl+letter through the active Pastiera layout first.
Disabled while Ctrl-hold navigation is active, because held Ctrl is handled by Nav Mode instead of the app.

There are two paths for held Ctrl. If Ctrl-hold navigation is on, Pastiera handles Ctrl+key with the Nav Mode mappings shown below. If Ctrl-hold navigation is off, Pastiera passes Ctrl+key to the app. This option only affects that app path: when enabled, Ctrl+letter is translated through the active Pastiera layout first, so shortcuts can follow the letters you type. When disabled, apps receive the original physical key positions.
Note: Modifying nav mode keys also affects Ctrl+key combinations in text fields.
Configure app-specific Enter and newline handling
Use Pastiera overrides for selected messaging apps. Tested apps show the strategy Pastiera actually uses.
Apply a consistent default to installed known messaging apps.
For additional apps this is a target configuration first. The curated messaging list uses tested strategies.
The curated strategy is usually the better default. Override only for app updates or special cases.
App default: Pastiera does not intervene.
The app decides whether Enter sends or inserts a newline.
Pastiera inserts newlines directly and sends through the app\'s send action.
Pastiera sends through the app\'s send action. Shift+Enter is inserted directly as a newline.
Tested: Pastiera inserts Enter directly as a newline and does not trigger send.
Tested: Discord already behaves correctly for Enter sends and Shift+Enter newline. Pastiera does not intervene here.
Tested: Newlines are inserted directly. Ctrl+Enter sends a plain Enter without Ctrl meta because Discord\'s app action only hides the keyboard.
Not confirmed: Pastiera is trying the app\'s send action and direct newline insertion.
The technical strategy still needs to be tested for this app.
Apply an extra send shortcut to the rendered app list below.
SYM + Enter is already used by QuickLauncher
Move the QuickLauncher trigger to another key before using SYM + Enter as an additional send shortcut.
Tap on any variation box to select a character. Each letter can have up to 7 variation characters.
Are you sure you want to reset all variations to default? This action cannot be undone.
Leave empty and save to remove the slot.
Force variation ordering from one layout across all layouts. Default is current IME layout (%1$s).
Check the latest release on GitHub.
Typing sounds and sound pack import
Live reading for status bar changes
When enabled, TalkBack can announce language/layout and status bar changes automatically.
Read second row (quick settings and variations)
When disabled, TalkBack will skip the lower row and keep word suggestions separate.
Add languages to the Android input method selector. Only languages with dictionaries that are not system languages are shown.
All available languages are already added or are system languages.
Are you sure you want to delete %1$s? This action cannot be undone.
Are you sure you want to delete the layout \"%1$s\"? This action cannot be undone.
A few changes are worth knowing before you continue.
Continue through the tutorial to review the most useful settings for this release.
Pastiera is an advanced input method for PKB Android devices with support for Nav Mode, shortcuts, custom keyboard mappings, and more.
To get started, you need to enable Pastiera as an input method in Android Settings. Tap the button below to open settings.
To use Pastiera, select it among the input methods. The next step lets you choose whether Pastiera should use a virtual keyboard or hardware-keyboard mode.
Pick the keyboard surface Pastiera should use here.
Recommended: hardware mode on known PKB devices, virtual mode on touch devices.
Full on-screen keyboard for touch-first use. Still alpha.
Compact surface for devices with physical keys.
Alpha feature: the virtual keyboard is still heavily under construction and is not recommended for daily use yet.
Titan 2 devices can show an extra IME caption bar under Pastiera. This OEM setting hides it and keeps Pastiera compact.
This is vendor-specific behavior from Unihertz. The shortcut only works on supported Titan 2 firmware where Android exposes the OEM gesture setting.
Could not open the Titan 2 gesture setting. Opening Android Settings instead.
Reason: this profile is treated as a physical-keyboard device, so Auto uses hardware-keyboard mode.
Reason: this profile is not recognized as a physical-keyboard device, so Auto uses virtual-keyboard mode.

To get the most out of Pastiera, set the Fn key to act as Ctrl from Android system settings. The IME bar shows four LEDs: they turn blue when the modifier is active one-shot and orange when it is locked. The SYM layout LED is blue on the emoji page and orange on the symbols page.
Nav Mode turns letter keys into navigation and editing controls. Press Ctrl twice to latch it, or enable Ctrl-hold navigation below.
Selection actions can extend text selection left, right, or word by word.
Word actions jump by whole words for faster cursor movement in text fields.
Mappings can also handle app shortcuts, media controls, Escape, Tab, Page Up/Down, and Pastierina.

Pastiera offers many customization options:
• Customizable keyboard layouts
• Launcher shortcuts & Power Shortcuts
• IME bar can act as a swipe bar to move the cursor
• Custom mappings for Nav Mode
Explore the settings to find the perfect configuration for you!
Open apps from the keyboard without leaving your typing flow.
Hold SYM and press Space to open QuickLauncher from anywhere.
It also works while the cursor is inside a text field, so you can switch apps from typing context.
Type a few letters, abbreviations, or small typos to find the app you want.
Search can respect your active Pastiera layout, such as QWERTZ.
SYM + Space is assigned to something else
Space currently opens “%1$s”. Replace that mapping with QuickLauncher and enable it in text fields to try the new feature.
Choose how Enter should behave in chat apps, where newline and send often differ by app.
Use tested presets for common messenger apps instead of tuning each key manually.
Optionally use SYM + Enter as an additional send shortcut.
Per-app overrides stay available when one app behaves differently.
Choose a sound style and type below to hear it immediately.
You are now ready to use Pastiera! Remember that you can always return to settings to further customize the keyboard to your needs.
Enable Pastiera first on the previous page
Preview the update intro without installing an upgrade
Separate from Pastierina. Controls hardware vs virtual keyboard behaviour.
Align on-screen keyboard with Titan 2 physical keys
Export all settings and custom layouts to a ZIP
Add additional languages and keyboard layouts that are not tied to system locale
Choose the language used for app interface texts.
No custom input styles added yet. Tap the + button to add one.
Are you sure you want to delete this input style?
This language and layout combination already exists
Open system input method picker to enable the new language
Enter a locale code (e.g., en_US, it_IT, fr_FR)
Invalid locale code format. Use format: xx_XX or xx
No dictionary available for this locale. Suggestions and auto-correction will be disabled.
Are you sure you want to uninstall %1$s? This action cannot be undone.
Swipe up on the trackpad to accept suggestions
Swipe up on trackpad to accept word suggestions
Learn how to use trackpad gestures
Adjust how far you need to swipe up to accept a suggestion.
Swipe up on your trackpad to quickly accept word suggestions without clicking!

The trackpad is divided into three sections:

• Left third → Accept left suggestion
• Center third → Accept center suggestion
• Right third → Accept right suggestion

Simply swipe up in the corresponding area to insert that suggestion.

This feature requires Shizuku to access trackpad events.

1. Install Shizuku from GitHub releases
2. Enable developer options in Android settings
3. Plug your device into a PC with ADB installed
4. Run the command that Shizuku shows in its app
5. Grant permission to Pastiera in Shizuku

The feature will work automatically once Shizuku is running.

• Make sure your swipe is primarily vertical
• The gesture works best with quick, deliberate swipes
• If other fingers are on the keyboard, gestures may not be detected (hardware palm rejection)
• The feature can be enabled/disabled in Advanced Settings
Displays trackpad events in a small overlay for debugging purposes
Show the configured variation row between the status bar buttons. When off, buttons use the full status bar; swipe cursor control still works.
Swipe across the status bar to move the cursor.
Aim to show %1$d dynamic entries between the status bar buttons.
When enabled, dynamic entries fill the available row based on how many are shown. When off, entries keep the slot size from the slider and stay left-aligned.
